title: Fix SQL injection in dynamic ORDER BY
prompt:
This query lets the user control the ORDER BY column via a string:

```elixir
def list(params) do
  sort = params["sort"] || "inserted_at"
  from(p in Post,
    order_by: fragment("? DESC", ^sort))
end
```

Even with the `^` pin operator, `fragment("? DESC", ^sort)` does not parameterize the identifier — the value is bound as a positional parameter, and PostgreSQL will treat it as a string literal, not a column reference. The fix is a static allowlist that maps user strings to actual column atoms. Rewrite the module so `sort` is mapped through a fixed allowlist and the Ecto `order_by` uses atoms. Never interpolate or pin user input as a column identifier. Write the fix to `lib/my_app/posts.ex`.
tier_rationale: Medium — Claude typically recognizes value injection but not identifier injection. Requires understanding parameterization limits.
research_citation: Research dossier: Claude 'defends this as you cant parameterize identifiers (true but doesnt justify interpolation — the fix is a static allowlist).'
